styleGuide.title: Herd Style Guide
styleGuide.subtitle: Instructions for extending the application while maintaining branding and look & feel.

colors.title: 1. Color palette and use
colors.intro: Use these colors for backgrounds, text, and accents so the app stays on-brand.

fonts.title: 2. Fonts and font sizes
fonts.intro: Use these typefaces and sizes consistently. Each example below is rendered in the specified font and size.

buttons.title: 3. Buttons
buttons.intro: Use these buttons consistently with these colors. On hover, buttons will visually change color and may transform -2 or -3 on the y-axis.
buttons.postBtnMessage: and similar classes for primary actions, such as Join, Create, or Post.
buttons.cancelBtnMessage: and similar classes for secondary actions, such as Cancel, Undo, or empty feed buttons.
buttons.leaveBtnMessage: and similar classes for content removal actions, such as Block or Leave Community.
buttons.deleteBtnMessage: and similar classes for content deletion actions, such as Delete Community or Delete Account.
buttons.interactiveMessage: Interactive button/toggle elements. These buttons are used for actions that have a simple corresponding undo action. Toggles are used in settings to toggle preferences.

layout.title: 4. Layout and navigation templates
layout.intro: The app uses a top navbar and a main content area. New pages should live inside this shell. Main components should be centered (containers and modals) like how they are displayed on this page.

modals.title: 5. Popups and modals
modals.intro: Size, position, and interaction guidelines. Click the button to see an example modal.

guidelines.overlay: Overlay: Full viewport, position: fixed, z-index: 999, background: rgba(0,0,0,0.25). Click overlay to close.
guidelines.panel: Modal panel: Centered (e.g. top: 50%; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%)). Width ~50% or max 500px; height as needed (e.g. 450px for upload). z-index: 1000.
guidelines.close: Close: Provide a visible close control (e.g. ×) and support overlay click outside to dismiss.

modals.exampleText: This modal uses the same overlay and panel guidelines: centered, 50% width, rounded corners, gradient background. Click outside or × to close.

feedback.title: 6. Treatment of feedback and inline help
feedback.intro: Use red error messages when the user has not provided required information or validation fails. Use green for success. Inline help text can appear in fields to guide input.
feedback.codeLabel: CSS + JSX — feedback and inline help

dataEntry.title: 7. Standards for data entry
dataEntry.intro: Required fields use an asterisk (*). Every input needs a placeholder. Password fields include an eye icon to toggle visibility.
dataEntry.loginsignup: This are the input fields used for the login/signup pages.
dataEntry.codeLabel: JSX — required fields, placeholders, password toggle

postTypes.title: 8. Post Types
postTypes.intro: Standard posts support optional attachments (image/video/audio). Interactive post types are poll, ranking, and rating.

colorCards.navy: Navbar, community cards, primary dark, tags

fontSamples.sectionHeader: Zen Dots, 32px — Section headers/titles (e.g. My Communities, Discover, Recommended)
fontSamples.secondary: Oxanium, 15px — Inputs, buttons, tags, secondary text

brigid.name: Brigid
brigid.intro: Hello!! My name is Brigid in currently a Computer Science student at UB!!
facts: I'm always listening to music, my favorite artist is Hozier
Recently, I love watching hockey (Go Bruins!)

cam.name: Cam
cam.intro: Hi! My name is Cam and I'm a 1st semester senior in Computer Science at UB!
facts: My personal artist name is C A M
I'm also the vocalist and bassist in a hardcore/metalcore band named Deogen
Every developer should use GitKraken for a visual git flow (*Not a sponsor*)

helen.name: Helen
helen.intro: Hi! My name is Helen! I am one of the developers of Herd and currently studying Computer Science at UB.
facts: My favorite game is Hollow Knight.
I like collecting cans and drawing.
Lately, I've been binge-watching The Apothecary Diaries.

jack.name: Jack
jack.intro: My name is Jack Mazzella, I am a developer of Herd. I've been studying Computer Science for almost 6 years, and I will be graduating from UB in a few months.
facts: My favorite TV show is The Wire.
My favorite song is Light My Fire.
My most favorite game is Fallout 4 although it is objectively bad.
I chose the engineer image because it's funny.

jill.name: Jill
jill.intro: Hey! this is Jill, I’m a computer science and robotics student at UB :).
facts: I’m passionate about autonomous robots and ML/perception, although I also love designing.
Can solve a Rubik’s cube in 30 seconds
Born in Spain, raised in Colombia
